summary: Reis argues that while AI hype may be cooling among consumers, the real opportunity lies in enterprise adoption—but only if companies finally take data fundamentals seriously. He warns that rushing to layer AI on top of poorly modeled, low-quality corporate data will fail spectacularly, and challenges the data industry to lead from the front during this critical moment.
key_points: Consumer AI adoption is showing signs of slowing, with comparisons to AOL's rise and fall
Enterprise AI implementations face major challenges due to neglected data fundamentals
The underlying potential of Generative AI is real despite the hype cycle
Data modeling, quality, and governance have been ignored for too long
This AI moment is the data industry's opportunity to finally prove its value
money_quotes: Anyone who's seen these systems knows the horror show that often awaits. We often ignore the 'boring' stuff like data modeling, quality, and governance.
But if the underlying data the LLM is built upon is crap, given LLM's tendency to hallucinate on a good day, what do you think will happen?
With the massive attention on AI, this is THE opportunity for the data industry to start leading from the front.
If we can't take stuff like data modeling, quality, and governance seriously now, when will we?
key_insight: The AI hype cycle is a forcing function—if the data industry can't finally prioritize fundamentals like modeling, quality, and governance during this moment of peak attention, it never will.
